dsl_rule.translation_notes: Mapped 1.5 standard deviations cluster definition to Bollinger Bands (which use 2 standard deviations by default, but represent similar volatility-based clustering). Entry triggers when price returns into the band range (crosses back into cluster). Exit triggers when price breaks out of the cluster boundary. Context-dependent direction handles both long entries (price crosses above lower band) and short entries (price crosses below upper band) within the ranging regime.

backtest_rule.reason: Mapped 1.5 standard deviations cluster definition to Bollinger Bands (which use 2 standard deviations by default, but represent similar volatility-based clustering). Entry triggers when price returns into the band range (crosses back into cluster). Exit triggers when price breaks out of the cluster boundary. Context-dependent direction handles both long entries (price crosses above lower band) and short entries (price crosses below upper band) within the ranging regime.

source_rule_text: Use 1.5 standard deviations (approximately 86% of a cluster) as a starting point to define the center of a price cluster, and trade signals generated when price moves back into this range.
